title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_11 × Z_11 × Z_2) prime?
generalization_signal: The six-class support-template for Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) survives the decisive feeder p = 11 with a clean C = 1 reduction and a {2,3}-smooth reservoir for A ∪ E; the same naive reservoir becomes exactly tight at p = 13, so the next honest question is refined arithmetic closure rather than more structural proof.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_11 × Z_11 × Z_2) survives skeptical verification and materially strengthens the active support-template campaign for Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2), but it remains an unformalized feeder result rather than an exact Lean-backed theorem.
candidate_theorem_slice: A sufficient-condition support-template theorem for odd primes p: after fixing C = 1, if {1,...,p^2+2p-2} contains 2(p-1) labels supported on a fixed small prime set S and another 2(p-1) labels coprime to every prime in S, then Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) is prime.
next_action: Run generalize on the Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) campaign using the verified p = 11 feeder, then choose between a p = 13 tight-packing pressure test and a family-level support-template writeup before any Lean work on this instance.
